growth_records.method: calculated: at chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.04 h-1; td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.04 = 17.33 h
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat, glucose 10%, 32 C, 100 h | L-leucine 24.8 +/- 0.2 g L-1 | L-alanine 0.8 +/- 0.1 g L-1 | yield 0.33 mol mol-1 | Productivity 1.9 g/L/h. Optimal dilution rate 0.04 h-1, C/N 57.6 (ammonium acetate)
growth_records.notes: Optimal chemostat condition (Table 3 + abstract). Reported experimental: L-leucine 24.8 g/L, L-alanine 0.8 g/L, yield 0.33 mol/mol, productivity 1.9 g/L/h, D=0.04 h-1, C/N 57.6 with ammonium acetate, T=32 C, pH 7.0-7.2. Steady-state growth: mu = D = 0.04 h-1 (chemostat identity), td = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. Derived glucose uptake (volumetric): qGlc_vol = D*(Sin-Sout) ~= 0.04*(100-5) = 3.80 g/L/h (Sin=100 g/L feed 'glucose 10%'; Sout assumed ~5 g/L as in fed-batch, residual for chemostat not explicitly stated). Derived L-alanine secretion = D*titer = 0.04*0.8 = 0.032 g/L/h. NOTE inconsistency: paper reports L-leucine productivity 1.9 g/L/h but D*titer = 0.04*24.8 = 0.99 g/L/h; the reported 1.9 is ~2x the D*titer value (their definition of productivity may differ) -- reported value retained but flagged. Steady-state DCW for the exact ammonium-acetate optimum not given in text; max steady-state DCW 39.4 g/L was with (NH4)2C2O4 at C/N 57.6, so specific (per-gDW) rates not computed to avoid mis-assignment. Confidence medium due to these unstated values.

growth_records.snippet: Prepared pre-cultures were inoculated (0.3 g CDW L-1) in 1.2 L CGXII medium supplemented with 12 g L-1 d-glucose as the sole carbon source. After 7.45 h of unlimited batch growth (mu = 0.42 h-1) added d-glucose was depleted entirely reachin

growth_records.method: reported/calculated (differential mu from CDW, viability-corrected; max mu reached 0.4 +/- 0.02 h-1)
growth_records.snippet: Growth acceleration in CGXII minimal medium + 5% glucose from mu=0 to 0.4 h-1; maximum growth rate reached was 0.4 +/- 0.02 h-1 (batch, 30 C, pH 7.4).
growth_records.notes: Transcriptomics/growth-modulon study. Batch bioreactor on modified CGXII (Buchholz et al 2014, composition NOT restated - cited) + 5% glucose. Viability-adjusted differential growth rates ranged 0 to 0.4 h-1 during acceleration; max mu=0.4+/-0.02 h-1 measured (transient, single acceleration curve; the 0.1/0.2/0.3 points are transient not distinct steady conditions). Doubling time at mu=0.4 = ln2/0.4 = 1.733 h. NO experimental substrate uptake/secretion rates measured for the main cultures. Only rate mentioned, uptPCA=2.04 mmol gCDW-1 h-1 (protocatechuic acid), is CITED from Unthan et al. 2014 (not measured here) and applies to the carbon-limited pre-culture. Pre-cultures used CGXII+1% glucose+30 mg/L PCA (C-limit), reduced K2HPO4/KH2PO4 0.01 g/L each (P-limit), or (NH4)2SO4 0.1 g/L + no urea (N-limit). 30 C, pH 7.4 via NH4OH.
